results.possibleIdentity: Robert E. Lamm was a mechanical and aeronautical engineer active during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was employed at the United Aircraft Research Laboratories (UARL) in East Hartford, Connecticut (which later became the United Technologies Research Center). He resided in Hebron, Connecticut. His technical expertise spanned mechanical engineering, aeronautics/aerospace, mathematics, and physics. Lamm was associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization established to facilitate collaborations between artists and engineers, where he was registered as a technical consultant or collaborator in their technical services database. He likely graduated from the Stevens Institute of Technology in 1966, where he was a member of the Beta Theta Pi fraternity. He may be the same Robert E. Lamm who later became a prominent figure in the Society of Motion Picture and Television Engineers (SMPTE) New England Section and founded or managed Cync Corp., a company specialized in motion imaging technology.
